nodes.task:
Fix server-side SSR/SEO bugs reported by audit-tradeupbot. Address GitHub issues #93, #94, #95, #100, #101, #102.

Issues (read each before coding):
- #93 [P0] Homepage HTML has 3 <title> tags and 2 <meta description> tags. The SSR prerender layer injects head tags AND React's client-side bundle injects them again — both end up in the response Googlebot sees. Fix so the SSR response contains exactly one <title> and one <meta name="description">.
- #94 [P0] sitemap-tradeups.xml lists individual /trade-ups/<id> URLs. Stale ids return 5xx (36 such errors in GSC). Choose Option A from the issue: stop emitting individual trade-up URLs in the sitemap entirely. The /trade-ups list page is the canonical entry point.
- #95 [P1] Blog post canonical tags are missing the trailing slash but the server 301-redirects /blog/<slug> → /blog/<slug>/. Make the rendered canonical URL match the served URL (include the trailing slash).
- #100 [P2] Collection sitemap (sitemap-collections.xml) includes sticker/agent/charm/graffiti/map collections that have zero trade-ups. Filter the sitemap so it only emits collections that contain skins eligible for trade-ups (e.g. has_knives || has_gloves || has_skin_rarities; whatever the existing data shape supports — inspect the route to find the right filter).
- #101 [P2] Homepage prerendered HTML shows '0 trade-ups', '0 profitable', '0 data points' because the counters animate client-side. Inject the real values from /api/global-stats (Redis-cached) into the SSR HTML so Googlebot sees real numbers.
- #102 [P2] Ensure all canonical URLs and sitemap entries use the non-www form (https://tradeupbot.app). Verify the SSR canonical is correct on home, /trade-ups, blog posts, collections.

Files to inspect/modify (repo-relative paths):
- server/seo.ts — primary SSR head/canonical/prerender layer; this is where dedupe and trailing-slash fixes belong.
- server/routes/sitemap.ts — sitemap generation for trade-ups + collections.
- server/index.ts — wiring; touch only if necessary.
- server/routes/data.ts or wherever /api/global-stats is defined — for #101, you'll likely call the same loader server-side rather than via HTTP.

Approach:
1. Read each issue body via `gh issue view <n>`.
2. Read server/seo.ts and server/routes/sitemap.ts in full before editing.
3. For #93, audit how the prerender injects head tags and either suppress duplicates from the client-side bundle path or strip duplicates in the prerender response. Confirm: `curl -A 'Googlebot' http://localhost:<port>/ | grep -c '<title>'` returns 1.
4. For #101, the cleanest path is to read the same global-stats source that /api/global-stats reads and inject the numbers as data attributes or directly into the rendered counter spans during prerender.
5. TDD: add/extend tests in tests/integration/seo.test.ts (create if missing) covering: single <title>, single <meta description>, blog canonical has trailing slash, sitemap-tradeups does NOT contain individual trade-up id URLs, sitemap-collections excludes empty/non-trade-up collections, homepage prerender contains a non-zero stat number. Use tests/helpers/fixtures.ts where applicable. Use `npx vitest run tests/integration/seo.test.ts` to verify.
6. Commit each fix as its own commit with a message like `seo: dedupe SSR <head> tags (refs #93)` — use `refs #N`, NOT `fixes #N` (do not auto-close).

Do not modify git config or push. End by signaling: flt workflow pass when tests are green and your coder.md handoff is written.

Fix Listing Sniper page bugs from audit-tradeupbot. Address GitHub issues #85, #90, #91.

Issues:
- #85 [P1] DMarket links for StatTrak items use `category_0=not_stattrak_tm`, which filters OUT StatTrak. Affected: every StatTrak result (Glock-18 Winterized, MP5-SD Liquidation, R8 Junk Yard, AK-47 Elite Build, Five-SeveN Nightshade, etc.). Fix so StatTrak items use `category_0=stattrak_tm` (or omit the filter). Make sure the non-StatTrak path remains correct.
- #90 [P2] Listing Sniper page is missing an H1 heading, a one-line description, and a page-specific <title>. Add: H1 'Listing Sniper' (or 'Underpriced Listings'), a short subtitle ('Listings priced below estimated market value, sorted by discount percentage.'), and set document.title to 'Listing Sniper | TradeUpBot' (use the same `useDocumentTitle` / Helmet pattern other pages use — grep src/pages/ for examples).
- #91 [P2] Top results are bogus when KNN has sparse history (e.g. MP7 Forest DDPAT BS at $0.08 estimated $95.00, +118,650% diff). Pick the cleanest fix from the issue: filter out listings whose estimate is unreliable. Recommended rule: hide results where KNN comparable count is below some threshold OR where estimated_value > 10x listing_price. Inspect server/routes/listing-sniper.ts for what fields are available — if comparable count/confidence is exposed, use it; otherwise apply the 10x cap. Sort/threshold should be applied server-side in the route, not client-side, to avoid leaking junk into the API response.

Files to inspect/modify:
- src/pages/ListingSniperPage.tsx — DMarket URL builder for #85, H1/title for #90, possibly client-side guard for #91.
- server/routes/listing-sniper.ts — backend filter for #91.
- Search for `category_0=not_stattrak_tm` across the repo (`grep -rn 'category_0=not_stattrak_tm' src server`) to find all places that build DMarket URLs — there may be a shared helper.

Approach:
1. `gh issue view 85`, `gh issue view 90`, `gh issue view 91`.
2. Find the StatTrak DMarket link builder. If it's a shared helper, add a stattrak boolean parameter and switch the category. If it's inline, fix it where it lives. Do not introduce a new abstraction just for this fix.
3. For #91, prefer adding the filter to server/routes/listing-sniper.ts where the listing list is built; this also reduces payload size.
4. TDD: add a unit test for the DMarket URL builder covering both stattrak and non-stattrak (tests/unit/dmarket-url.test.ts or wherever the helper lives — colocate with existing tests). For #91, add a unit/integration test that the filter excludes listings whose estimate exceeds 10x the listing price (or whatever rule you implement). Run with `npx vitest run`.
5. Commit as separate commits: one per issue, message format `listing-sniper: <summary> (refs #N)`. Use `refs #N`, never `fixes #N`.

Do not modify git config or push. End by signaling: flt workflow pass when tests are green and your coder.md handoff is written.

Fix landing-page UX/SEO bugs from audit-tradeupbot. Address GitHub issues #87, #88, #92, #98, #99, #103.

Issues:
- #87 [P2] Discord link inconsistency. Landing page (src/pages/LandingPage.tsx:433) uses `https://discord.gg/tradeupbot`. Site footer (src/components/SiteFooter.tsx:32) uses `https://discord.gg/gQ8cPqBq2a`. Pick the one that points to the live server (ask via `gh issue view 87` for any clarification in comments; default to the one used by all other pages: `https://discord.gg/gQ8cPqBq2a`) and unify all references.
- #88 [P2] At 375px viewport, landing-page nav hides Features/Pricing/FAQ/Blog with no hamburger fallback. Add a hamburger/dropdown menu that exposes those links on small viewports. Use the existing tailwind/shadcn primitives the project already imports (check src/components/ui/ if a Sheet or DropdownMenu exists). Don't invent a new menu pattern.
- #92 [P3] Landing-page nav 'Blog' uses `href="#blog"` (scrolls to a homepage section). It should navigate to `/blog`. Same applies to the landing-page footer 'Blog' link, and to Features/Pricing/FAQ links if they are hash anchors in the nav (those should point to /features, /pricing, /faq pages — see #99).
- #98 [P1] Marketing screenshots (collections.png 559KB, expanded.png 411KB, dataviewer.png 345KB, tradeuptable.png 228KB) are huge unoptimized PNGs. Skip the WebP conversion (cwebp may not be available in the worktree). Instead, do the markup-only fixes: add `loading="lazy"` to images that are below the fold, add explicit `width` and `height` attributes (read actual dimensions with `file public/<image>.png` or by inspection — these are documented as 1280x... type screenshots; if exact dims aren't quickly verifiable, omit the attribute rather than guessing). Note in your handoff that WebP conversion is left for a follow-up.
- #99 [P2] Hash-fragment URLs (/#blog /#faq /#features /#pricing) appear as separate pages in GSC. Combined with #92, fix all landing-page nav AND footer links so they point to /features, /pricing, /faq, /blog (real pages) rather than #features, #pricing, #faq, #blog. Search src/pages/LandingPage.tsx for `href="#`.
- #103 [P2] Hero CTA 'View Trade-Ups' is a `<button>` so Googlebot can't follow it. Convert it to an `<a href="/trade-ups">` styled as a button (or use react-router's `<Link to="/trade-ups">`). Preserve the existing visual styling.

Files to inspect/modify:
- src/pages/LandingPage.tsx — primary file for all six issues.
- src/components/SiteNav.tsx — shared nav, may need hamburger work that benefits more than the landing page.
- src/components/SiteFooter.tsx — Discord link unification.
- src/components/ui/* — check for existing Sheet/DropdownMenu before adding deps.

Approach:
1. `gh issue view 87 88 92 98 99 103` (one at a time).
2. Read src/pages/LandingPage.tsx in full first; many of these fixes are in the same regions.
3. Make edits in groups (nav links, footer links, hero CTA, image attributes, mobile menu).
4. TDD: add a small RTL/vitest test if reasonable (e.g. tests/unit/landing-page-links.test.ts asserting nav anchors have hrefs with leading `/` not `#`). For the mobile menu, an existence test (rendered hamburger button at small viewport) is enough; do not test interaction details.
5. Run `npx vitest run` and `npx tsc --noEmit`.
6. Commit one logical commit per issue with `refs #N` (not `fixes #N`).

Do not modify git config or push. End by signaling: flt workflow pass when tests are green and your coder.md handoff is written.

Fix scattered UI bugs from audit-tradeupbot. Address GitHub issues #84, #86, #89.

Issues:
- #84 [P1] Blank 404 page. Visiting any unknown URL renders just nav+footer with empty content. Inspect src/App.tsx (~line 444-458) — the catch-all `<Route path="*" element={<AuthGatedApp />} />` falls through silently. Create src/pages/NotFoundPage.tsx with a clear message ('Page not found') and a link back to /trade-ups. Wire it as the catch-all inside the inner Routes (and inside AuthGatedApp's Routes too if needed). Also set the response status code if the SSR layer cares about that — check server/seo.ts.
- #86 [P2] 'knifes' typo on /collections cards. The fix is in src/components/CollectionListViewer.tsx around line 155: change `knife{c.knife_type_count !== 1 ? 's' : ''}` to `${c.knife_type_count === 1 ? 'knife' : 'knives'}`. Verify no other 'knifes' occurrences via `grep -rn 'knifes' src/`.
- #89 [P2] /my-trade-ups renders the landing page when unauthenticated (because of the catch-all in App.tsx). Inside src/pages/MyTradeUpsPage.tsx, gate the page on auth state. If unauthenticated, render a sign-in prompt: 'Sign in with Steam to see your trade-ups' with a button linking to `/auth/steam?return=/my-trade-ups`. Find existing auth context/hook by grepping for `useAuth`, `isAuthenticated`, or `currentUser` in src/.

Files to inspect/modify:
- src/App.tsx — routing for #84.
- src/pages/NotFoundPage.tsx — CREATE for #84.
- src/components/CollectionListViewer.tsx — #86.
- src/pages/MyTradeUpsPage.tsx — #89.
- (auth context wherever it lives) — read-only for #89.

Approach:
1. `gh issue view 84 86 89`.
2. Read src/App.tsx, src/pages/MyTradeUpsPage.tsx, src/components/CollectionListViewer.tsx.
3. For #84, make sure the NotFound route does not break the existing catch-all logic — `AuthGatedApp` may have its own internal Routes that need a 404 too.
4. TDD where reasonable: tests/unit/collection-list-viewer.test.ts asserting 'knives' for count > 1 and 'knife' for count === 1. The 404 and auth-gate are component routing tests — skip if not straightforward in the existing test setup.
5. Run `npx vitest run` and `npx tsc --noEmit`.
6. Commit one logical commit per issue with `refs #N` (not `fixes #N`).

Do not modify git config or push. End by signaling: flt workflow pass when tests are green and your coder.md handoff is written.

Fix content/SEO copy bugs from audit-tradeupbot. Address GitHub issues #96, #97.

Issues:
- #96 [P1] /trade-ups main page ranks at position 50. Add an H2 intro paragraph above (or below, depending on layout) the trade-up table that targets primary query variations: 'Find profitable CS2 trade-up contracts from real, buyable listings across all rarity tiers — Knife, Glove, Covert, Classified, Restricted, Mil-Spec.' Also add a short FAQ section at the bottom of the page with 2-3 Q&As targeting position 40-65 queries (suggested: 'What is a CS2 trade-up contract?', 'How does TradeUpBot find profitable trade-ups?', 'Are these listings live?'). Keep the tone analytical/educational, not hype (per project preference). Inspect src/pages/TradeUpsPage.tsx to find the right insertion points. Make sure the new content is part of the SSR output so crawlers see it.
- #97 [P1] Two blog post titles need updates to include the specific data users are searching for:
  - cs2-trade-up-marketplace-fees: Title → 'CSFloat Seller Fee (2%), DMarket & Skinport: CS2 Trade-Up Marketplace Costs | TradeUpBot'. Description → 'CSFloat charges a 2% seller fee + buyer fee. DMarket: 2% seller + 2.5% buyer. Skinport: 12% seller. See how these fees affect your CS2 trade-up profitability.'
  - cs2-float-formula (or similarly-named float guide post — find via `grep -rn 'Adjusted Floats' content/ src/`): Title should incorporate 'cs2 float ranges', 'what is float in cs2', 'what is adjusted float cs2'. Suggest something like 'CS2 Float Ranges Explained: How Adjusted Float Actually Works | TradeUpBot'. Verify the description front-loads the answer.

Files to inspect/modify:
- src/pages/TradeUpsPage.tsx — intro + FAQ for #96.
- content/blog/<slug>.md or content/blog/<slug>.mdx (or wherever blog posts live; check `ls content/`) — frontmatter title/description for #97.
- If titles are also injected via server/seo.ts based on slug, double-check that the SSR head reflects the updated frontmatter.

Approach:
1. `gh issue view 96 97`.
2. `ls content/` and read the two blog post files in full before editing.
3. Update frontmatter title/description for both blog posts.
4. Add intro + FAQ to TradeUpsPage.tsx. Keep it concise — analytical tone, no marketing fluff.
5. TDD is light here (copy changes). If there's an existing snapshot or SSR test, update it. Otherwise, manual verification via `curl -A 'Googlebot' http://localhost:<port>/trade-ups | grep '<h2>'` after starting the server is enough — note this in your handoff.
6. Run `npx tsc --noEmit` and `npx vitest run`.
7. Commit per issue: `content: <summary> (refs #96)` and `content: <summary> (refs #97)` — use `refs`, never `fixes`.

Do not modify git config or push. End by signaling: flt workflow pass when tests are green and your coder.md handoff is written.

Fix bundle/perf issues from audit-tradeupbot. Address GitHub issue #104.

Issue:
- #104 [P2] No code splitting — single 195KB gzipped JS bundle. Enable route-based splitting via React.lazy + Suspense in src/App.tsx. The Routes block is at src/App.tsx:363 (inner) and :444 (outer). Convert at least the heavyweight pages to lazy imports: Calculator, ListingSniper, Skins/SkinPage, MyTradeUps, BlogPost, TradeUpShare. Keep small, always-needed components (LandingPage, SiteNav, SiteFooter) eagerly imported so the initial paint isn't blocked. Wrap the Routes block in a single <Suspense fallback={<...>}> with a minimal loader (use whatever loading indicator already exists; do not introduce a new one).

Do NOT do the manualChunks variant — React.lazy gives correct route-keyed chunks by default with Vite.

Files to inspect/modify:
- src/App.tsx — primary.
- vite.config.ts — only touch if a chunk-naming hint is needed (probably not).

Approach:
1. `gh issue view 104`.
2. Read src/App.tsx end-to-end first; this file has nested Routes and a layout split that you must preserve.
3. Coordinate with the ui-misc node: it adds a NotFoundPage and may also touch routing. This node depends_on ui-misc, so its changes will already be merged when this node runs — read the latest src/App.tsx.
4. Convert imports: e.g. `const CalculatorPage = lazy(() => import('./pages/CalculatorPage'))`. Wrap Routes in <Suspense>.
5. Verify build: `npm run build` — confirm chunked output (multiple JS files in dist/assets, main bundle smaller than before). Note approximate sizes in your handoff.
6. Run `npx tsc --noEmit` and `npx vitest run`.
7. Commit: `perf: route-based code splitting (refs #104)` — `refs`, not `fixes`.

Do not modify git config or push. End by signaling: flt workflow pass when tests are green and your coder.md handoff is written.
